mappings.rationale: Covers the structural and physiological characteristics that set humans apart in the biosphere, including bipedalism, brain size, and long development.
Directly addresses the effects of human action upon the biosphere, including humanity's modification of the environment at a planetary scale.
Covers a major consequence of human influence on the biosphere, directly relevant to the modification of the environment by the human species.
Directly covers the conservation and management of natural resources, including management of wildlife and habitat preservation.
Directly covers domestication and cultivation of plants and animals, plant breeding, animal breeding, and food production.
Directly covers attempts to change genetic endowments through deliberate selective measures as outlined in section B.3.
Addresses the economic dimensions of natural resource management and the utilization of organisms by humans.
Covers the ethical framework for human stewardship of the biosphere and conservation management of natural resources.
Addresses the political dimensions of conservation management and the governance of human impacts on the biosphere.
Covers the legal frameworks for conservation and management of natural resources.
Directly relevant to conservation management and the sustainable utilization of natural resources by humans.
Addresses management of the world's food supply including food security challenges facing developing countries.
Relevant to management of the world's food supply and the nutritional dimensions of food production and distribution.
Provides the ecological context for understanding human impacts on the biosphere and the nature of conservation management.
Covers forest resources as a major type of natural resource subject to conservation management and human utilization.
Covers human behavioural capacity and culture, including communication through speech and the unique cultural adaptations of humans.
Covers a major consequence of human modification of the environment through species introductions and habitat alteration.
Relevant to understanding how the management of natural resources intersects with global politics and territorial control.
Addresses ethical considerations in the human utilization of animals, including domestication and zoological collections.
Covers the care of domesticated animals, relevant to animal cultivation and the maintenance of animal collections.
